name: Easy Cheese and Shrimp Gyozas (East/west)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Medium shrimp (41 to 40 per pound) shelled and deveined
1 tsp Finely minced ginger or ginger juice
2 tsp Dry sherry or rice wine
1 1/4 cup Grated Monterey Jack cheese (about 5 ounces)
1 package Round siu mai wrappers or wonton wrappers
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle salt and white pepper to taste, along with sprigs of fresh coriander.
2. Toss the shrimp with 1 teaspoon of salt and let stand for 10 minutes. Rinse thoroughly, drain, and pat dry.
3. Finely chop the shrimp and add them to a mixing bowl.
4. Add remaining salt, ginger, rice wine, cornstarch, water chestnuts, green onions, coriander, sausage, and cheese to the mixture; mix thoroughly.
5. If using wonton wrappers, trim the corner to make a circle.
6. Place 1 heaping teaspoon of filling in the center of 1 wrapper. Moisten the edge with water and fold in half to enclose the filling, forming a half-circle. Pinch the edges together to seal.
7. Set on a baking sheet; cover with a towel. Repeat with remaining filling and wrappers.
8. Cook and sauce for 4 servings:
- Add 1 tablespoon of oil to a 10-inch nonstick skillet and set over medium heat.
- Arrange 12 gyozas in one single layer; pan-fry for 1 minute or until lightly browned. Turn the gyozas over to brown the other side, about 1 minute longer.
- Add chicken stock, shake the pan to prevent the gyozas from sticking. Cover and cook at a low boil for 2 minutes. Remove gyozas to a plate; keep warm.
- Increase heat under the skillet to high, add cream; bring to a boil, stirring for 45 seconds until thickened. Stir in lime juice.
- Season with salt and pepper.
9. To serve, divide the sauce among 4 plates. Arrange 3 gyozas on each plate. Garnish with fresh coriander sprigs.
Note: There is enough filling to make 4 dozen gyozas. The amount of sauce indicated above is enough for 12 gyozas. If you want to make more, multiply the sauce ingredients, but remember that you will have to make the sauce separately as part of each batch.
Freeze extra uncooked gyozas on a baking sheet; when frozen, transfer them to a freezer bag. Do not defrost before browning.
Makes 4 dozen gyozas. Serves 16.
